page: Amazon Web Services m7g.medium is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by a custom AWS Graviton processor. Built on the 64-bit ARMv8 architecture, specifically utilizing the Neoverse-V1 core design, this instance features 1 dedicated physical core and 1 vCPU running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The processor is equipped with 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, includes no local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the single-core performance of the m7g.medium falls within the average range, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1509.38 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1418.0. However, because the instance is limited to a single vCPU, its multi-core performance is constrained, resulting in poor ratings across multi-threaded tests, such as a Geekbench multi-core score of 1417.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 771.73. Memory subsystem benchmarks show a read-only small block bandwidth of 96.94 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 10.71 GB/sec. Single-threaded decompression tasks perform moderately well, with a bzip3 score of 9.09 GB/sec, while more complex operations like software compilation and database operations show lower relative performance.

The m7g.medium offers an entry-level point into the ARM-based Graviton ecosystem, providing dedicated CPU resources rather than burstable shared-core allocations. The primary tradeoff is the strict single-core limitation, which restricts its utility for parallelized workloads, multi-threaded applications, or heavy database operations. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance provides a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio for lightweight, single-threaded tasks without the overhead of larger multi-core instances. It is suitable for low-traffic static web serving, lightweight single-threaded background workers, basic text processing, and small-scale development or testing environments that do not require local NVMe storage or high network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services m7g.medium is a general-purpose instance featuring 1 dedicated vCPU and 1 physical core powered by an ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, 32 MB of L3 cache, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1418.0, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are limited due to the single-vCPU configuration. This instance is designed for lightweight, single-threaded workloads such as basic web serving, small-scale development environments, and simple text processing tasks where dedicated CPU allocation is preferred over burstable resources.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.medium is a general-purpose ARM64 instance featuring 1 dedicated Graviton vCPU, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, suitable for lightweight single-threaded tasks.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.medium is a general-purpose ARM64 instance with 1 dedicated Graviton vCPU, 4.0 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A single-core ARM64 instance with dedicated CPU allocation and balanced memory for lightweight, single-threaded workloads and development environments.
bullet_points: Powered by a single-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARMv8 processor at 2.6 GHz
Configured with 4.0 GB of system memory and 32 MB of L3 cache
Delivers 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Features dedicated CPU allocation with no local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and development tasks
